headline: BIS warns AI frenzy could trigger market slump
detail: The central bank of central banks is publicly cautioning that AI-driven speculation could destabilize markets and the broader economy. This is direct headline risk for your entire portfolio - AVGO, PLTR, CRWD, SMH, QQQ are all priced with AI growth embedded. If institutional risk managers react to this, your names are in the crosshairs.

headline: Severe weather threatening AI infrastructure boom
detail: Physical disruptions to data centers and power grids are emerging as a real constraint on the AI buildout story. AVGO and SMH are heavily tied to AI infrastructure spending - anything that clouds that capex cycle is a headwind for both names right now when they're already on sell signals.

headline: South Korea: Samsung and SK Hynix investing in AI semiconductor mega-projects
detail: This is a mild positive signal for the broader chip sector and supports SMH's HOLD status. Continued aggressive investment from memory giants confirms the AI chip cycle isn't dead, just volatile - some reassurance for your SMH position which is still up 10%.

headline: China widens Japan export curbs targeting tech and defense
detail: Escalating tech trade restrictions between China and Japan add geopolitical friction to the semiconductor supply chain. SMH and AVGO both have exposure to Asia-Pacific supply chains, and tightening curbs historically spook chip stocks even when the direct revenue hit is unclear.
